utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 of these items explicitly. Each item is fully specified: every coverage type (health, property, travel, additional services) is present, clearly labeled, and includes costs (€535.0/month bundled). The total_budget item also provides the monthly and annual breakdown (€535.0/month or €6420.0/year). Therefore, all required items are included and fully specified with details and costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Plan33' for all categories—health (physical and mental), property, travel, and additional services. The ground-truth dataset contains 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' for health and travel, and 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' for property. The suffix '33' appears to be a variant of the same 'LifeFirst Standard' plan family, so these correspondences apply. For health coverage (physical and mental health insurance), the plan maps to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under both physical_health and mental_health, each rated 8. For property coverage, it maps to 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under home_content, rated 8. For travel coverage, it maps to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under travel_insurance, rated 7. For additional services, since 'LifeFirst Standard Plan33' bundles concierge services and global assistance, the closest match in the ground-truth is the travel_enhancement_services 'Concierge Services', rated 7. Therefore, each of the chosen components can be mapped to these ratings.
